2604.11306.title: Learning to Forget -- Hierarchical Episodic Memory for Lifelong Robot Deployment
2604.11306.paper_summary: Robots must verbalize their past experiences when users ask "Where did you put my keys?" or "Why did the task fail?" Yet maintaining life-long episodic memory (EM) from continuous multimodal perception quickly exceeds storage limits and makes real-time query impractical, calling for selective forgetting that adapts to users' notions of relevance. We present H$^2$-EMV, a framework enabling humanoids to learn what to remember through user interaction. Our approach incrementally constructs hierarchical EM, selectively forgets using language-model-based relevance estimation conditioned on learned natural-language rules, and updates these rules given user feedback about forgotten details. Evaluations on simulated household tasks and 20.5-hour-long real-world recordings from ARMAR-7 demonstrate that H$^2$-EMV maintains question-answering accuracy while reducing memory size by 45% and query-time compute by 35%. Critically, performance improves over time - accuracy increases 70% in second-round queries by adapting to user-specific priorities - demonstrating that learned forgetting enables scalable, personalized EM for long-term human-robot collaboration.
2604.11306.paper_summary_zh: 機器人必須在用戶詢問「你把我的鑰匙放在哪裡？」或「為什麼任務失敗？」時，口頭表達他們的過去經驗。然而，從持續的多模態感知中維持終身的情節記憶（EM）很快就會超出存儲限制，並使實時查詢變得不切實際，這需要一種適應用戶相關性觀念的選擇性遺忘。我們提出了H$^2$-EMV，一個使人形機器人能夠通過用戶互動學習記住什麼的框架。我們的方法逐步構建層次化的情節記憶，利用基於語言模型的相關性評估進行選擇性遺忘，這些評估是基於學習的自然語言規則進行的，並根據用戶對遺忘細節的反饋來更新這些規則。在模擬家庭任務和來自ARMAR-7的20.5小時實際錄音的評估中，H$^2$-EMV在保持問答準確性的同時，將記憶大小減少了45%，查詢時間計算減少了35%。關鍵是，性能隨著時間的推移而改善 - 在第二輪查詢中，準確性提高了70%，這是通過適應用戶特定的優先事項實現的 - 這表明學習的遺忘使得可擴展的個性化情節記憶成為長期人機協作的可能。
2604.11306.author: Leonard Bärmann et.al.
2604.11306.authors: Leonard Bärmann, Joana Plewnia, Alex Waibel, Tamim Asfour
